name: Resource strain and burnout
description: Community and school workers fill structural gaps without proper staffing or training.

name: Disconnection between funders and frontline
description: Funders focus on visibility; frontline workers face human consequences.

name: Listening as Trust-Building
description: Embedding genuine listening practices into policy design allows institutions to capture lived experiences, not just statistics. By hearing voices like Luke’s and Seán’s, government can rebuild trust and legitimacy, turning policies into solutions that feel real and relevant to people’s daily lives.

name: Shift funding toward prevention and partnership
description: By embedding lifestyle and community-based strategies into care, funders can make prevention part of the system.

name: Fragile volunteer infrastructure
description: Sports and wellbeing programmes depend on exhausted volunteers, threatening sustainability.

name: Recognise sport as a health asset
description: Investing in sport as an inclusive mental health entry point can turn physical activity into a preventive tool.

name: Fragmented care between mind and body
description: Patients’ physical health deteriorates because services operate in silos and overlook holistic wellbeing.

name: Chronic underfunding of community work
description: Local organisations face systemic problems with only temporary, project-based funding.

name: Invest in long-term community capacity
description: Sustained funding and support networks can rebuild community trust and reduce isolation.

name: Reactive, not preventive systems
description: Youth services constantly address crises instead of building prevention mechanisms.

name: Build mentoring and peer-support ecosystems
description: Social connections and mentoring help young people feel valued, safe, and resilient.

name: Overloaded schools acting as mental health providers
description: Teachers and counsellors are filling a mental health gap without training or institutional support.

name: Early intervention within schools
description: Embedding trauma-informed support at younger ages can prevent future mental health crises.

name: Addressing mental health as a systemic issue, not individual
description: Poverty and instability drive youth distress, not individual weakness.

name: Short-termism and fragmented systems
description: The funding and service model prioritises projects over sustained systems change.
